channel.title: Defense
channel.description: News, Analysis and Opinion from POLITICO

item.title: Hegseth gutted offices that would have probed Iran school strike
item.description: The number of Pentagon employees who focus on mitigating civilian casualties has dropped from 200 people to less than 40.

media:content.media:title: A woman reaches out to the coffins during funeral of mostly children killed in what Iranian officials said was an Israeli-U.S. strike Feb. 28 on a girls' elementary school in Minab, Iran, Tuesday, March 3, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Jack Detsch

item.title: Pentagon says 140 troops wounded in Operation Epic Fury
item.description: The update provides the clearest accounting so far of U.S. casualties tied to the Iran conflict, which has involved the deaths of seven U.S. troops as of Monday.

media:content.media:title: An Army carry team moves the flag-draped transfer case containing the remains of U.S. Army soldier Maj. Sorffly Davius, of Cambria Heights, N.Y., who died in Kuwait, during a casualty return at Dover Air Force Base, Delaware, on March 9, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Joe Gould and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Joe Gould and Paul McLeary

item.title: For Iraq and Afghanistan veterans, Iran feels like déjà vu
item.description: Post-9/11 veterans say the U.S. strikes on Iran feel like the opening chapter of a war they thought was over.

media:content.media:title: Army veteran Christopher William McFarland protests the war in Iran on March 2, 2026, in Clarksville, Tennessee.

item.dc:creator: By Riya Misra
item.dc:contributor: By Riya Misra

item.title: Allies fear Iran war will leave them without US weapons they bought
item.description: European and Asian countries worry the Pentagon is burning through munitions so fast that it won’t have enough to send the weapons they have purchased.

media:content.media:title: Kids walk around an Iranian missile after it fell near Qamishli International Airport, near the Turkish border in Hasakah, Syria on March 4, 2026, amid the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, John Sakellariadis and Phelim Kine
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, John Sakellariadis and Phelim Kine

item.title: Trump’s war aim: Iran’s ‘unconditional surrender’
item.description: The president's message — coming after days of mixed signals from administration officials — articulates that the U.S. has no intention of providing Tehran with an off-ramp.

media:content.media:title: In a Truth Social post, President Donald Trump wrote that “there will be no deal with Iran except UNCONDITIONAL SURRENDER!”

item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing

item.title: GOP restrainers say Trump abandoned them
item.description: The president’s restraint-minded backers accuse the administration of breaking its promise.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth (center) and White House deputy chief of staff Stephen Miller pose for a group photo with officials and security leaders from across Latin America and the Caribbean at the inaugural Americas Counter Cartel Conference in Doral, Florida, on March 5.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Eli Stokols and Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Eli Stokols and Paul McLeary

item.title: Congress declines to halt Iran war
item.description: Lawmakers will effectively be spectators in the president’s decisions around the Iran war after both the House and Senate rejected efforts to curb Trump’s power.

media:content.media:title: This image provided by U.S. Central Command shows a Navy sailor signaling the launch of a F/A-18F Super Hornet aircraft on the USS Gerald R. Ford (CVN 78), in support of Operation Epic Fury on Feb. 28, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: Harvard sidesteps Hegseth’s ban on military students
item.description: The school will allow troops accepted into its academic programs to defer enrollment for four years after Pentagon leaders barred studies there.

media:content.media:title: Harvard University will allow military students new options to defer or continue their education after the Pentagon cut academic ties with the school.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III

item.title: Senate rejects bid to scale back Iran war
item.description: A similar measure is also expected to fail in the House, giving the president near carte blanche to proceed with the conflict.

media:content.media:title: Iraqi Shiite carry a replica of a coffin of Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, who was killed by a U.S. airstrike in Tehran, during a symbolic funeral, in Najaf, Iraq on March 4, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien

item.title: War with Iran has ‘only just begun,’ Hegseth says
item.description: Top military officials said the campaign will intensify — and declined to say what victory might look like.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks during a news conference at the Pentagon on March 2, 2026, in Arlington, Virginia.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Lawmakers splinter on Trump administration’s case for war on Iran
item.description: The administration made its case behind closed doors on Tuesday — and Republicans liked what they heard.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Lindsey Graham speaks with reporters after a briefing from Trump administration officials on the U.S. strikes on Iran at the U.S. Capitol on March 3.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien, Joe Gould and Leo Shane III
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien, Joe Gould and Leo Shane III

item.title: Trump is running out of time to sell his rationale for Iran war
item.description: Senior officials keep offering justifications for the conflict with Tehran that the president upends.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump in the Rose Garden of the White House after stepping off Marine One in Washington, on March 1, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Myah Ward, Felicia Schwartz, Alex Gangitano and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Myah Ward, Felicia Schwartz, Alex Gangitano and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Noem defends her leadership of DHS amid sharp questioning from senators
item.description: An unexpected Republican joined in holding Trump’s embattled DHS chief to the fire.

media:content.media:title: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem arrives to testify during a Senate Judiciary Committee hearing on Capitol Hill in Washington, on March 3, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Eric Bazail-Eimil
item.dc:contributor: By Eric Bazail-Eimil

item.title: Dems use Iran to attack Trump on economy
item.description: The minority party hopes to link the administration’s military operation with domestic issues.

media:content.media:title: U.S. Rep. Jason Crow (D-Colo.) speaks to reporters at the Capitol on Dec. 16, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Joe Gould and Connor O'Brien
item.dc:contributor: By Joe Gould and Connor O'Brien

item.title: Iran strikes aren’t an ‘endless’ war, Pentagon policy chief argues
item.description: Elbridge Colby’s rejection of an open-ended conflict comes as the administration seeks to solidify its messaging on the U.S. operation in Iran.

media:content.media:title: Pentagon policy chief Elbridge Colby testifies before the Senate Armed Services Committee on Capitol Hill, on March 3, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Joe Gould
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Joe Gould

item.title: Trump to POLITICO: Iran is ’running out of launchers’
item.description: He also said U.S. defense companies are “under emergency orders” to build weaponry.

media:content.media:title: President Donald Trump speaks during a Medal of Honor Ceremony in the East Room of the White House in Washington, on March 2, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Sophia Cai
item.dc:contributor: By Sophia Cai

item.title: GOP has the votes to crush Iran war rebuke, Johnson predicts
item.description: Trump administration officials will brief lawmakers Tuesday ahead of high-stakes war powers votes this week.

media:content.media:title: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaks with reporters as he arrives to brief lawmakers on the U.S. strikes on Iran at the U.S. Capitol on March 2.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Jack Detsch
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Jack Detsch

item.title: Hegseth doesn’t rule out US troops in Iran
item.description: The nation’s top military officials made the case for why the U.S. needed to attack now.

media:content.media:title: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth speaks during a press conference on U.S. military action in Iran, at the Pentagon in Washington, on March 2, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Pentagon offers no evidence to support claim it attacked Iran in defense
item.description: The Trump administration’s effort to construct a case for war after shots started flying has few historical parallels.

media:content.media:title: An Arleigh Burke-class guided-missile destroyer fires a Tomahawk land attack missile in support of Operation Epic Fury on March 1, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch, Joe Gould, Eli Stokols and John Sakellariadis
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch, Joe Gould, Eli Stokols and John Sakellariadis

item.title: 3 US troops killed in Iran attack
item.description: Several more troops were wounded as part of the U.S. and Israel effort against Tehran.

media:content.media:title: A plume of smoke rises near Erbil International Airport in Erbil, Iraq, on March 1, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Ben Johansen
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Ben Johansen

item.title: Iranian supreme leader killed in airstrike, leaving power vacuum atop regime
item.description: The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei could dramatically affect power dynamics in the Middle East.

media:content.media:credit: Office of the Iranian Supreme Leader via AP
media:content.media:title: Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei listens to a speaker in a meeting in Tehran, Iran, on Oct. 20, 2025.

item.dc:creator: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Jack Detsch, Nahal Toosi and Joe Gould
item.dc:contributor: By Giselle Ruhiyyih Ewing, Jack Detsch, Nahal Toosi and Joe Gould

item.title: A duo of the willing: US and Israel have few allies in initial Iran strikes
item.description: The absence of allies in America’s massive attacks against Iran upends decades of precedent.

media:content.media:credit: Mahsa/Middle East Images/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:title: A plume of smoke rises over Tehran after a reported explosion on Feb. 28, 2026, after Israel and U.S. strikes.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary, Jack Detsch and Nahal Toosi
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary, Jack Detsch and Nahal Toosi

item.title: Dems vow to force votes on Iran war powers next week
item.description: The sponsors of the war powers legislation, Reps. Ro Khanna and Thomas Massie, had initially planned to hold a vote this week.

media:content.media:title: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.), flanked by House Minority Whip Katherine Clark (D-Mass.) and House Democratic Caucus Chair Pete Aguilar (D-Calif.), speaks with reporters at the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 8, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Connor O'Brien and Meredith Lee Hill
item.dc:contributor: By Connor O'Brien and Meredith Lee Hill

item.title: Iran strikes threaten to deplete US weapons supplies — and put American troops at risk
item.description: The administration already burned through significant stockpiles of air defense missiles in operations against Iran and its proxy groups.

media:content.media:title: US Airforce KC-46 Pegasus tankers and one P-8 Poseidon tankersfrom the US Navy are seen stationed at Lajes Air Base in the Azores on February 23, 2026. The United States has intensified its use of the air base amid tensions with Iran.

item.dc:creator: By Jack Detsch and Joe Gould
item.dc:contributor: By Jack Detsch and Joe Gould

item.title: What happened after Elon Musk took the Russian army offline
item.description: From a Ukrainian listening post, our reporter heard it happen: Starlink terminals went dark, leaving Russian soldiers along the front unable to coordinate.

media:content.media:title: Drone footage gathered by the Ukrainian military shows widespread battlefield use of Starlink terminals by Russian troops.

item.dc:creator: By Ibrahim Naber
item.dc:contributor: By Ibrahim Naber

item.title: Hegseth revives push to punish Sen. Mark Kelly
item.description: The Defense secretary appealed a ruling this month in favor of the former Navy captain.

media:content.media:title: Sens. Ruben Gallego (D-Ariz.) and Mark Kelly (D-Ariz) depart a press conference before the State of the Union address at the U.S. Capitol in Washington, on Feb. 24, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Leo Shane III, Connor O'Brien and Kyle Cheney
item.dc:contributor: By Leo Shane III, Connor O'Brien and Kyle Cheney

item.title: Trump’s Iran buildup risks drowning out affordability message
item.description: The massive accumulation of aircraft carriers and fighter jets in the Middle East, coupled with the Pentagon’s Venezuela operation, could cost billions of dollars.

media:content.media:credit: Illustration by Bill Kuchman/POLITICO (source images via Getty Images and Tajh Payne/US Navy via Getty Images)
media:content.media:title: Main.McLearyGould.IranBuildup green.jpg

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary and Joe Gould
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary and Joe Gould

item.title: Newsom says Trump’s attacks are bringing Europe together
item.description: It’s the president’s one contribution to the continent, the California governor said.

media:content.media:title: California Gov. Gavin Newsom prepares to address the audience at the Munich Security Conference in Munich, Germany, on Feb. 13, 2026.

item.dc:creator: By Gregory Svirnovskiy
item.dc:contributor: By Gregory Svirnovskiy

item.title: US ambassador to NATO: America is 'not leaving' Europe
item.description: His comments are part of a more conciliatory tone American officials are taking during the Munich Security Conference.

media:content.media:description: US NATO Ambassador Whitaker: The US is 'not abandoning Europe'
media:content.media:title: US NATO Ambassador Whitaker: The US is 'not abandoning Europe'

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary

item.title: Top Republican lawmakers spar over Greenland
item.description: Without naming him directly, Sen. Thom Tillis appeared to fire back at Sen. Lindsey Graham over his comments on Greenland's sovereignty.

media:content.media:title: Sen. Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) speaks to reporters before a weekly Republican policy luncheon at the U.S. Capitol on June 3, 2025, in Washington.

item.dc:creator: By Paul McLeary
item.dc:contributor: By Paul McLeary
